predictions.reasoning: Diary entry 'Talking with my best friend' is the dominant signal — unambiguous positive social interaction. Behavioral data aligns: indoor, stationary, moderate keyboard activity (521 chars, 33 sessions) consistent with active messaging/conversation. User profile shows high social support (81) making best-friend contact a high-value positive event. PHQ-8=13 and GAD-7=8 suggest baseline depression/anxiety, but today's social context suppresses NA below personal norm. Positive affect elevated above baseline; negative affect low; low ER need during enjoyable social engagement.

Diary is the dominant signal: 'My family is home / Dinner time with my boys' is a peak positive moment for this participant — baseline history confirms family dinners are his primary source of joy and coping. Behavioral data fully aligns: 0 min screen time in evening (fully present with family), 100% stationary (settled at home), indoor, only 105 chars typed. Morning was also socially positive (talking with best friend, ER_desire=1). Moderate depression/anxiety profile (PHQ-8=13, GAD-7=8) prevents full zeroing of negative affect, but the situation is strongly positive. ER desire is minimal — family provides natural emotional regulation. Intervention not appropriate at dinner time with family.

Diary signals genuine comfort and prosocial engagement: cat warmth (direct coping mechanism noted in baseline profile) + school volunteering (purposeful social activity). No negative markers whatsoever. Walking spike (z=+13.72) confirms physical outing consistent with volunteering; sedentary/indoor pattern afterward consistent with relaxing at home with cat. Session memory shows ER_desire=1 on both prior EMAs — strong pattern of low regulation need. Most similar past day (Nov 20 sim=0.93) also showed ER=1. Afternoon context (relaxed home, cat in lap) resembles the Nov 20 available=yes slot. PHQ-8=13/GAD-7=8 introduce some background NA floor but diary provides no active signal of anxiety or depression today.

Diary 'Dinner time with my family' signals warm, positive family engagement — consistent with this participant's strong family-centric coping pattern. Behavioral data (stationary, indoor, social app use) fully aligns with a home family evening. Most similar historical day (2023-11-20, similarity=0.93) had identical diary theme and yielded ER_desire=1, INT_availability=no. Baseline depression (PHQ-8=13) and anxiety (GAD-7=8) contribute moderate background NA, but no acute stressors are present. Currently occupied with family dinner → unavailable for intervention.

Diary 'Almost slept through the night' signals a mildly positive morning — near-complete sleep is meaningful for a cancer survivor with moderate depression (PHQ-8=13). No pain, worry, or stress signals. Behavioral data shows very high screen use by 9 AM (241 opens, 2h28m, Z=+22.55 vs morning baseline), dominated by social apps (1h10m), suggesting active morning engagement rather than distress — possibly catching up on social media after waking. Motion is near-sedentary (4m walking), consistent with quiet home morning. Prior session pattern shows consistently low ER_desire (0-1); morning EMA context with no family obligations indicated → availability is yes. Confidence moderate due to sparse diary entry.

Diary 'Day with family. No work!' is a clear positive signal — family is this participant's documented primary joy source, and explicit relief from work reinforces positive affect. Behavior is fully consistent: mostly stationary indoors, 1h33m driving (family outings), dominant social app use, elevated screen time vs baseline (relaxed leisure). Both most behaviorally-similar past days are family evenings with INT_availability=no. Morning EMA today was also no. Family immersion on a dedicated family day strongly predicts non-availability for intervention. ER_desire=1 consistent with his near-zero pattern throughout study.

Third EMA of the day; all three show family focus with ER_desire=0. 'Day with family' diary is brief and unambiguously positive — no stressors, no pain, no work. Behavioral profile aligns: social apps dominant (1h10m), some driving (family outing), relaxed stationary indoor day. Both most-similar past days (sim=0.94, 0.76) were evening family entries with INT=no and low ER_desire. Every prior evening EMA for this participant was INT_availability=no. Background PHQ-8=13 anxiety acknowledged but no behavioral or diary signal today. High confidence in positive state and non-availability.

Thanksgiving morning — diary entry brief but holiday context is strong. This family-centered person (recurring diary themes of family dinners, game nights) is likely in warm anticipatory state ahead of holiday gathering. Behavioral data shows first fully stationary day this week (0 min walking/driving) — at-home holiday. 40 min social apps and elevated keyboard activity (2060 chars, highest this week) suggest family communication. Screen time (172 min) consistent with prior days, no anomalies. PHQ-8=13 indicates moderate depression risk but recent ER_desire has been consistently 0, suggesting emotional stability. Thanksgiving context activates grateful/pleased/happy states. Morning EMA pattern + holiday family engagement → not receptive to interventions. INT_availability=no consistent with morning EMAs and occupied family context.

Thanksgiving afternoon EMA. Diary 'Family time' is brief and warm — consistent with this participant's pattern of positive family-engagement entries. Full-day stationary behavior confirms stay-at-home holiday; social app use (40 min) aligns with holiday messaging. Session history shows consistently low ER_desire (=0) since 11/21 and afternoon INT_availability reliably = yes. Thanksgiving context strongly signals gratitude and cheerfulness. PHQ-8=13 tempers PA ceiling; background anxiety possible but not signaled by diary or behavior today. High cross-modal consistency supports confident positive-state prediction.

Thanksgiving evening — participant has been with family all day (diary: 'Thanksgiving day' morning, 'Family time' midday and evening). Completely stationary, indoor, moderate screen use consistent with home family gathering. All three behaviorally similar days (similarity 0.87–0.98) were family-at-home days with ER_desire=0–1 and INT_availability=no. Every evening family EMA this week shows INT_availability=no. Diary is brief but positive with no stressors, pain, or anxiety signals. Thanksgiving context strongly implies grateful, pleased, happy affect. PHQ-8=13 suggests moderate baseline depression but no acute signal today. High confidence on INT_availability=no and low ER_desire from the consistent within-person pattern.

Day after Thanksgiving; diary 'Family time' is identical to prior day afternoon entry (11/23 13:47) which had INT_availability=yes. Every afternoon EMA in session memory (13:38, 14:00, 13:52, 13:47) shows availability=yes — strong time-of-day pattern. ER_desire=0 consistent across 9/10 recent EMAs during this family holiday week. Behavioral data (mostly stationary/indoor, 2h screen, elevated walking) aligns with relaxed family day. PHQ-8=13 moderate depression but no acute stressors surfaced. Thanksgiving holiday context supports grateful/pleased/cheerful states.

Evening EMA on day-after-Thanksgiving. Diary 'Family time' matches 11/23 evening identically (similarity=1.00). All 4/4 behaviorally similar days returned INT_availability=no with ER_desire=0. Every evening EMA this week has been unavailable — consistent with family dinner time pattern. Behavioral profile (indoor, stationary, moderate screen/keyboard) confirms relaxed family day at home. Holiday context (Thanksgiving weekend) amplifies positive affect and gratitude. PHQ-8=13 baseline depression moderates ceiling on PANAS_Pos; no acute stressors visible.

Thanksgiving holiday weekend evening — 4th consecutive 'Family time' diary entry. Behavioral profile is relaxed/stationary indoor day fully consistent with family gathering. All 3 most similar past days show ER_desire=0, INT=no. Every prior evening EMA in session memory shows INT_availability=no; ER_desire=0 for all EMAs since 11/21. The EMA 44 min ago at 19:04 also showed ER_desire=0, INT=no. Strong convergent evidence for positive affect, minimal negative affect, no regulation need, and family-engaged unavailability.

Diary 'Cat snuggles' is a warm, low-arousal positive signal consistent with this person's documented coping pattern (pet comfort). Post-Thanksgiving Saturday morning — relaxed, stationary, social apps dominant. All 3 most similar days show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=no. Every morning EMA in session history (09:00) results in INT_availability=no. Thirteen consecutive EMAs with ER_desire=0 reinforces low emotional regulation need. Mild-positive affect state, no stressors visible.

Diary 'Family time' is this participant's signature Thanksgiving weekend entry, used identically on 11/23, 11/24, and now 11/25 evening. All 4 most behaviorally similar past days show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=no with family-themed diaries. Every evening EMA in 14-entry session history yields INT_availability=no — a near-perfect personal pattern. PHQ-8=13 baseline depression means PA won't peak, but context (Thanksgiving, family present, no stressors mentioned) supports moderate-high positive affect and minimal negative affect. Zero signal of pain, worry, or ER need today.

Diary describes quiet solo time before family wakes on a Sunday morning — neutral-to-positive, no distress. Behavioral profile (fully stationary indoors, moderate screen/social use) is classic restful home day, consistent with Thanksgiving-week family pattern. All 3 most similar days show ER_desire=0, INT_available=no. Every morning EMA in session history returns INT_availability=no. Moderate PA (peaceful contentment) with minimal NA (PHQ-8=13 baseline depression acknowledged but no active distress signals).

Diary 'Cleaning house' is task-focused with no emotional language — neutral-to-mild productive affect on day-after-Thanksgiving. Motion sensors show full stationarity (unusual) but consistent with light indoor domestic work not captured by accelerometer. Moderate screen use with social apps (33 min) suggests periodic breaks/connection-seeking. Morning EMA showed ER_desire=1 ('Time before family is awake'), slightly elevated from this person's typical 0, but afternoon cleaning activity suggests occupied/busy state reducing emotional regulation desire. Baseline PHQ-8=13/GAD-7=8 contribute modest background NA, but no acute stressors. Thanksgiving week family context (consistently positive in diary all week) provides mild positive affect foundation. Busy cleaning activity → INT_availability=no.

Diary: 'Family time' — this participant's canonical positive evening entry, repeated across 7+ prior evening EMAs. Behavioral profile perfectly matches prior family days: stationary, indoor, moderate social app use (33m), no work signals. All 3 nearest similar days (sim=0.98–0.99) show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=no. Evening EMAs with family context show 100% consistency for unavailability. Today's morning ER=1 spike ('Time before family is awake') did not persist into the afternoon/evening. Low NA, moderate-high PA, not receptive to intervention.

Diary 'Back to work and hurting' is unambiguous: pain + first workday after Thanksgiving. Behavioral data confirms commute (51m driving), pain-limited sedentariness (4m walking, 20h+ stationary), and elevated screen use (z=+13.49, likely work catch-up or stress scrolling). All 4 prior morning EMAs showed INT_availability=no; at work while hurting reinforces unavailability. ER_desire elevated above baseline due to explicit pain report — historically 0-1, bumped to 4 given 'hurting'. Negative affect elevated across pain, sadness, worry (PHQ-8=13, GAD-7=8 profile). No positive signals in diary or behavior.

Diary explicitly states 'overwhelming' on first workday post-Thanksgiving, pairing with morning 'hurting' (ER_desire=1). Screen opens are extremely elevated (227, Z=+13.55 vs personal baseline) — frequent brief checks indicating restlessness/anxiety. Stationary, indoor, keyboard-active profile confirms desk-bound work day. Moderate depression (PHQ-8=13) and anxiety (GAD-7=8) lower the threshold for overwhelm. Transition from a warm family-holiday week to demanding work is a well-documented stressor for this individual. Similar-day behavioral profiles are misleading (all family-time days); diary clearly diverges. Pain likely persists from morning. High NA, low PA; ER_desire elevated but INT_availability historically 'no' during work hours.

Today is the first full work day returning post-Thanksgiving holiday. Same-day diary escalates from 'hurting' (morning, ER=1) to 'overwhelming' (afternoon, ER=5) to 'very long work day' (evening). Behavioral data fully corroborates: 20h sedentary, indoor, moderate keyboard/screen consistent with desk work. Pain confirmed in morning, work overwhelm in afternoon. ER_desire estimated ~6 given sustained escalation. All evening EMAs in session memory show INT_availability=no — pattern is consistent. PA suppressed by exhaustion; NA elevated by pain+stress+work overwhelm; PHQ-8=13 supports elevated negative affect floor.

Diary 'Exercise and work' signals a coping attempt (exercise) against ongoing work stress from yesterday's overwhelming return (ER_desire=5). Screen time is at the top 1% of this person's mornings (Z=+12.30), suggesting heightened anxiety/hypervigilance rather than calm. Walking slightly elevated, confirming exercise. Driving indicates commuting to work. PHQ-8=13 and GAD-7=8 underlie chronic depressive/anxious baseline. Exercise provides some positive affect lift (PA_State=true), but work stress carryover keeps NA elevated. ER_desire reduced from yesterday's peak (5→2) as exercise serves as partial coping. INT_availability=no is near-universal for this person, especially on workday mornings (20/21 past observations). Similar behavioral days match holiday patterns but emotional context is clearly work-stress.

Diary 'Car pickup and work' is terse and task-focused — characteristic of this person under work stress (vs. warm family-time entries). Session memory shows escalating ER_desire (1→5→5→6→5) since returning to work post-Thanksgiving, with explicit 'overwhelming' and 'hurting' language. Behavioral data confirms: 99 min driving (car pickup errand), screen use dramatically elevated vs. personal afternoon baseline (Z=+11.76), minimal social app use — consistent with stress-driven phone checking and social withdrawal. PHQ-8=13 baseline depression + ongoing physical pain signal (11-27: 'hurting') supports elevated NA, pain_State=true. Afternoon work context + consistent pattern of INT_availability=no during all work periods → unavailable.

Morning EMA with participant actively preparing for Physical Therapy — a purposeful response to the physical pain and 'hurting' documented since returning to work on 11-27. ER_desire elevated above their typical baseline (most entries = 0) but reduced from the 11-27/28 peak (5-6) as they've moved into action-coping mode. Pain is the dominant signal given the PT context and recent complaints. The 2h+ driving in behavioral data is consistent with an appointment trip. Similar-day matches (Thanksgiving family days) should be discounted — behavioral similarity is superficial given starkly different diary context. INT_availability=no: actively heading to an appointment early morning.

Diary 'Physical therapy' + morning 'Prepping for PT' confirms a medical appointment day. Behavior aligns: 2h+ driving to/from PT, high screen opens (272, frequency-checking behavior), elevated screen time vs prior days. PA is dampened (PHQ-8=13 moderate depression, trait pattern). Worry about PT effectiveness is a documented recurring theme. Pain state is likely given PT context and 'hurting' noted on 11/27. ER_desire decreases slightly post-PT from morning baseline of 3 to ~2, as the appointment anxiety resolves. INT_availability=no consistent with appointment context and all similar-day patterns. ER_desire_State=true because 2 > typical 0-1 baseline for this person.

Diary 'Family time with headache' signals mixed state: social warmth (family) + physical pain (headache). Earlier today: PT with ER_desire=5, suggesting physical fatigue and discomfort persisting into evening. Behavioral data confirms post-PT resting pattern (14h45m stationary, minimal walking), elevated screen use (Z=+32), social apps aligning with family engagement. Similar past days (Thanksgiving family time) showed ER_desire=0 and no pain — today differs meaningfully due to explicit headache and PT-day stress. Moderate PA from family connection, elevated NA from pain and work-return fatigue (ER_desire 5-6 on 11/27-11/28). ER_desire estimated 4 (midday was 5, evening pain persists). Pain, worried, and forecasting states elevated per person's anxiety baseline (GAD-7=8) and health concerns. INT_availability=no based on consistent pattern across all 8+ prior evening EMAs.

Diary unambiguously signals acute physical pain: 'Woke up with a migraine' — a direct report of debilitating pain at day onset, consistent with recurring migraine pattern in baseline. Behavioral data provides perfect cross-modal confirmation: zero walking all day (100% stationary across all time blocks, vs baseline 0.7 min/hr), 248 fragmented screen opens suggesting repeated brief phone checks while incapacitated (light sensitivity prevents sustained screen use), entirely indoor. This follows an escalating 3-day physical/emotional strain trajectory: return-to-work stress Nov 27 (ER=5,5), exercise+work Nov 28 (ER=6), PT Nov 29 (ER=5), headache evening Nov 29, migraine morning Nov 30. PHQ-8=13 and GAD-7=8 amplify pain-related distress. ER_desire elevated (6/10) reflecting desire for relief from pain and anticipatory stress about managing the workday. INT_availability=no based on consistent historical non-receptivity pattern (0/27 prior EMAs resulted in receptive=yes) combined with active migraine state.

Diary unambiguously describes physical illness and functional impairment, directly continuing from morning's migraine (ER_desire=8). Behavioral data fully corroborates: 100% stationary all day, 0 min walking, screen use Z=+23 (typical sick-day phone-scrolling). This person went from holiday rest → stressful work return (11/27-28, ER 5-6) → PT + headache (11/29) → migraine (morning) → still sick afternoon. High negative affect, pain state, and ER desire are all indicated. Completely sedentary and homebound = no availability for intervention (consistent with all prior EMAs since 11/26).

Diary 'Family time' is a consistently positive signal for this participant — all prior evening family time entries show ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=no. However, today's context is important: the person started with a migraine (ER=8) and was feeling sick in the afternoon (ER=6). The evening shift to 'Family time' suggests meaningful recovery and comfort through family support, but residual physical effects likely remain (pain_State=true). Behavioral data confirms a day of near-complete inactivity consistent with illness. All 3 similar behavioral days were non-receptive. Positive affect elevated (family connection), negative affect reduced from its peak but not zero (post-illness evening), ER_desire drops to low levels consistent with the comfort-seeking having been fulfilled. Not available due to family engagement — consistent pattern across all evening family time entries.

Diary 'Time with husband' signals a warm, intimate morning with partner — positive and socially connected. This follows a difficult week (migraine, work stress, PT), making this restorative moment more emotionally salient. Behavioral data shows a sedentary indoor day with moderate social app use, consistent with a quiet at-home morning together. Similar days in history (family time patterns) all show ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=no, strongly predicting low distress and non-availability. Mild background anxiety expected given PHQ-8=13 and cancer survivor context, but the diary's positive relational content dominates the signal.

Diary 'Lots of work' is brief and matter-of-fact — less distressed than Nov 27's 'overwhelming' work entries (ER=5). Behavioral data confirms heavy work engagement: screen time far above personal baseline (z=+18), high afternoon keyboard activity (1833 chars), sedentary and indoor. No pain or physical complaints unlike prior sick/migraine days. Work afternoon pattern strongly predicts INT_availability=no (consistent across all prior work afternoon EMAs). ER_desire estimated ~3: some desire for regulation from work load but not acute distress. Moderate PA (morning positive, balanced trait scores) with mild work-related worry (GAD-7=8 background). Interactions quality elevated from morning spouse time.

Evening 'Family dinner' diary maps perfectly onto a well-established behavioral signature: all 3 most similar past days and every prior evening family entry show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=no. No pain mentioned today (contrast with 11/29 headache and 11/30 migraine). Stationary indoor behavior confirms home-based social evening. High confidence given 10+ identical prior observations.

Diary 'Family time' is this participant's most consistent evening entry. Across 12+ similar past EMA instances, this pattern invariably yields ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=no. Morning EMA with self-reported poor sleep. Diary is brief and neutral-negative — fatigue/tiredness implied but no acute pain, worry, or major stressor. Overnight sensor window shows no screen/motion data (device off), consistent with being in bed. Full-day behavioral narrative is active and normal-range, suggesting poor sleep did not cascade into significant distress. NA mildly elevated, PA below personal norm from reduced energy.

Diary 'Baking with family' signals warm, positive social bonding — a clear emotional recovery from morning poor sleep (ER_desire=6 at 8:52am). Behavioral profile (stationary at home, social apps dominant at 1h11m) is fully consistent with a family-at-home evening. Five most similar past days all show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=no — this is the stable evening family pattern for participant 071. High social support (81) and family as primary coping resource further support low NA and elevated PA. Evening timing + family presence = not available for intervention.

Diary 'Didn't sleep. Pain' signals severe distress — full sleep deprivation plus explicit physical pain in a cancer survivor with PHQ-8=13. Behavioral data strongly corroborates: screen_on_min=207 (z=+29.63, top 1% of his mornings) is the unmistakable footprint of a sleepless night spent on the phone. Nearly stationary (6 min walking) consistent with pain limiting mobility. Historical anchors: migraine morning (11-30) yielded ER_desire=8; yesterday's 'didn't sleep well' yielded ER_desire=6. Today combines both, escalating to ER_desire=8. All morning EMAs with pain/distress in this participant's history show INT_availability=no. Cancer context amplifies fear/forecasting given unexplained pain. High NA state (miserable, worried, afraid, sad, lonely) with suppressed PA.

Diary directly signals work overload and obligation stress ('too much to do'). Morning EMA (ER=7, pain, no sleep) establishes a high-distress baseline for the day. ER_desire estimated ~5 (above personal mean but below morning's acute 7). INT_availability almost invariably 'no' — work context reinforces this strongly.

Diary reveals a day of cascading distress: morning no-sleep/pain (ER=7), midday work overwhelm (ER=6), and evening 'hard news about next semester' — a future-facing stressor compounding an already burdened day. Cancer survivor context amplifies the weight of 'hard news'; in combination with work overwhelm and physical pain, this person is at peak negative affect for the day. Behavioral data corroborates: 267 screen opens suggest anxious checking, social app use (47min) points to seeking social support after receiving news, and near-total sedentariness reflects emotional heaviness. Keyboard activity (792 chars afternoon, 375 evening) suggests active communication about the news. Pain from morning likely persists into evening. 'Next semester' framing activates forecasting/worry. Evening INT_availability=no follows overwhelming historical pattern — this person consistently marks evenings unavailable regardless of ER_desire (ER=5 Nov 27 evening, ER=5 Nov 27 midday both = no in evening context).

Diary 'Coffee with a friend' signals a genuine positive social moment — a clear upswing from the Dec 4 trifecta of pain/work-overwhelm/hard news (ER=7,6,8). Underlying PHQ-8=13 and recent bad news prevent top-tier PA; moderate positive/low negative prediction. INT_availability=yes consistent with this person's strong recent pattern of marking available (Dec 1-4 all yes, even during stress).

Diary 'Busy at work' signals moderate work engagement without acute distress — a marked improvement from yesterday's high-ER crisis entries (pain, overwhelming obligations, hard news). Morning's positive social coffee likely buffered current affect. Behavioral data confirms active work day: screen use very elevated vs baseline, moderate keyboard activity, long drive (commute/errands). Walking unusually high, possibly a stress-relief break. Moderate NA retained due to PHQ-8=13 baseline depression and background work stress; PA maintained from morning social positivity.

Evening 'Family time' diary — a pattern repeated 8+ times in session history, always yielding ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=no. Diary 'Therapy not working. More appointments not good news' is unambiguously distressing — therapy failure combined with explicit bad medical news represents a high-NA, low-PA moment. Driving 2h49m behaviorally corroborates the appointments. High-frequency screen checking (217 opens) signals anxiety/rumination. ER_desire calibrated against prior high-distress entries (migraine ER=8, hard news ER=8). Pain_State=true given morning PT + ongoing physical pain history. Diary 'Attending a DAR meeting' signals structured civic-social engagement — a meaningful break from recent health crisis (therapy failure, bad news 12/6, ER_desire=6 this morning). Social activity with an organized group provides a positive affect boost for this person who has high social support (81) and moderate extraversion. Diary reveals mixed state: family time provides positive social grounding, but therapy complications (bad news Dec 6, ongoing worry Dec 7-8) create persistent background anxiety and worry. 'Still on my mind' signals rumination rather than acute crisis — ER desire is moderate, not high. Diary 'Kids birthday party' is unambiguously positive — celebration, family, social warmth. Day-long positive pattern: morning husband snuggles, afternoon family time, evening party. Driving (72 min) confirms travel to party venue; elevated screen use consistent with photo-taking/social sharing.
